questions.explanation: High pitch = high frequency = waves bunched closer together.
questions.hint: Pitch changes how close the waves are.

questions.q: Why does a sound become quieter the further you are from the source, even with no obstacles?
options: The wave's energy spreads over a larger area, so the amplitude reaching you is smaller
questions.explanation: Energy spreads out as the wave travels, reducing amplitude (loudness) with distance.
questions.hint: Think about the energy spreading out.

questions.q: A sperm cell has a tail. This adaptation helps it to…
questions.explanation: The tail (flagellum) lets the sperm swim towards the egg.
questions.hint: How does it reach the egg?

questions.q: Metals are found where on the periodic table?
questions.explanation: Metals are on the left and middle; non-metals on the right.
questions.hint: Non-metals are on the right.

questions.q: Which statement about the nucleus is correct?
options: It contains DNA that controls the cell
It is only in plant cells
questions.explanation: The nucleus holds DNA and controls cell activities; it is in both animal and plant cells.
questions.hint: Control centre with genetic info.

questions.q: Which is a renewable energy resource?
questions.explanation: Wind is renewable — it won't run out.
questions.hint: Which one keeps being replaced naturally?

questions.q: Why are there usually far fewer top predators than producers in an ecosystem?
options: Energy is lost at each level, so less is available higher up
questions.explanation: Only ~10% of energy passes up each level, so there is much less energy to support top predators.
questions.hint: Think about energy loss up the chain.

questions.q: Why can a gas be compressed but a solid cannot?
options: Gas particles have big spaces between them
questions.explanation: Gas particles have large gaps that can be squeezed; solid particles are already touching.
questions.hint: Think about the spaces between particles.

questions.q: Which correctly orders these from smallest to largest?
questions.explanation: One species (population) → all species (community) → community + surroundings (ecosystem).
questions.hint: Start with one species.

questions.q: Why does a mixture not have a fixed melting point, while a pure substance does?
options: The different substances in a mixture melt at different temperatures, giving a range
questions.explanation: Each component melts at its own temperature, so a mixture melts over a range; a pure substance has one fixed point.
questions.hint: Think about each component melting separately.

questions.q: Which technique separates the colours in black ink?
questions.explanation: Chromatography separates coloured dyes.
questions.hint: Used for inks and dyes.

questions.q: In a parallel circuit the main current is 0.6 A and splits equally between two identical branches. Current in each branch?
questions.explanation: Current splits between branches: 0.6 A ÷ 2 = 0.3 A each.
questions.hint: It splits between the branches.

questions.q: An element is a substance made of…
questions.explanation: An element contains only one type of atom.
questions.hint: One type of atom only.

questions.q: On which axis do you plot the dependent variable?
questions.explanation: Dependent → Responding → Y-axis (DRY).
questions.hint: Remember DRY MIX.

questions.q: An echo is…
options: Sound reflected off a hard surface
questions.explanation: An echo is reflected sound.
questions.hint: Sound bouncing back.

questions.q: When you heat a solid, the particles…
questions.explanation: Heating gives particles more energy so they vibrate faster (the particles do not change size).
questions.hint: More energy = more movement.

questions.q: Which set-up gives the most valid (fair) test of how light affects plant growth?
options: Different plants, different pots, different water
Same plant type, same water and soil, only light changed
Measure a different plant each day
questions.explanation: Only the independent variable (light) should change; everything else is controlled.
questions.hint: Change one thing only.

questions.q: A balance reads 2 g when nothing is on it. This is an example of…
questions.explanation: Equipment not reading zero when empty is a zero error.
questions.hint: What should it read when empty?

questions.q: Roughly how much energy passes to the next level of a food chain?
questions.explanation: Only about 10% passes on; the rest is lost as heat, movement and waste.
questions.hint: It's a small fraction.

questions.q: Which is a categoric variable?
questions.explanation: Type of fruit is a label/word, so it is categoric.
questions.hint: Which one is a word, not a number?

questions.q: A polar bear's white fur is an adaptation for…
questions.explanation: White fur camouflages the bear against snow when hunting.
questions.hint: How does it stay hidden?

questions.q: Which structure gives a plant cell its shape and support?
questions.explanation: The cellulose cell wall provides support and shape.
questions.hint: Made of cellulose.

questions.q: Which material is an insulator?
questions.explanation: Plastic does not conduct electricity — it is an insulator.
questions.hint: What coats electrical wires for safety?

questions.q: The unit of voltage is the…
questions.explanation: Voltage is measured in volts (V).
questions.hint: Volt-age.

questions.q: Fertilisation is the joining of…
options: A sperm nucleus and an egg nucleus
questions.explanation: Fertilisation joins a sperm nucleus with an egg nucleus to form a zygote.
questions.hint: One from each parent.

questions.q: Asexual reproduction produces offspring that are…
questions.explanation: Asexual reproduction has one parent and produces identical clones.
questions.hint: One parent, no mixing of genes.

questions.q: 50 g of salt is added to water and stirred; only 36 g dissolves and 14 g sits at the bottom. What can you conclude?
options: The solution is saturated at that temperature
questions.explanation: Undissolved solid remaining means no more can dissolve — the solution is saturated.
questions.hint: Why won't the last 14 g dissolve?

questions.q: Which statement about evaporation and boiling is correct?
options: Both happen only at the surface
Boiling happens throughout the liquid at the boiling point; evaporation happens at the surface at any temperature
Evaporation needs a higher temperature than boiling
questions.explanation: Boiling occurs throughout at the boiling point; evaporation is a surface effect at any temperature.
questions.hint: Where does each one happen in the liquid?

questions.q: 'Accurate' means a result is…
questions.explanation: Accurate = close to the true/real value.
questions.hint: Bullseye.

questions.q: Gas → liquid is called…
questions.explanation: Gas to liquid is condensing.
questions.hint: Like steam on a cold window.

questions.q: Roughly how long is a human pregnancy (gestation)?
questions.explanation: Human gestation is about 9 months.
questions.hint: About three-quarters of a year.

questions.q: In a series circuit, if one bulb breaks, the other bulbs…
questions.explanation: Breaking the single loop stops the current, so all bulbs go out.
questions.hint: There's only one loop.

questions.q: Why is it more energy-efficient for humans to eat plants directly than to eat animals that ate the plants?
options: Each step up a food chain loses ~90% of the energy, so eating lower down wastes less
questions.explanation: Eating at a lower trophic level avoids the large energy losses between levels.
questions.hint: Energy is lost at each level you go up.

mocks.title: Paper 1 — Mock 6
mocks.instructions: 60 multiple-choice questions. Answer all. 45 minutes. 1 mark each.

questions.q: Which improvement would best increase the accuracy (not just precision) of a timing experiment using a stopwatch?
options: Use light gates instead of a hand-held stopwatch
Record more decimal places by guessing
questions.explanation: Light gates remove human reaction-time error (a systematic/random human error), improving accuracy. Repeats only reduce random error.
questions.hint: What causes the timing error in the first place?

questions.q: On which axis do you plot the dependent variable?
questions.explanation: Dependent → Responding → Y-axis (DRY).
questions.hint: Remember DRY MIX.

questions.q: An image of a cell measures 50 mm wide under ×500 magnification. What is the cell's real width?
questions.explanation: Real size = image size ÷ magnification = 50 ÷ 500 = 0.1 mm.
questions.hint: Real size = image ÷ magnification.

questions.q: In a parallel circuit, if one branch bulb breaks, the others…
questions.explanation: Other branches still have a complete loop, so they stay on.
questions.hint: Each branch is separate.

questions.q: Which energy transfer happens in a torch (flashlight)?
questions.explanation: Chemical store in the battery transfers electrically to the bulb, then to light (and wasted heat).
questions.hint: Start with the battery's store.

questions.q: What is the chemical symbol for sodium?
questions.explanation: Sodium's symbol is Na.
questions.hint: It comes from its Latin name natrium.

questions.q: Roughly how much energy passes to the next level of a food chain?
questions.explanation: Only about 10% passes on; the rest is lost as heat, movement and waste.
questions.hint: It's a small fraction.

questions.q: Which set-up gives the most valid (fair) test of how light affects plant growth?
options: Different plants, different pots, different water
Same plant type, same water and soil, only light changed
Measure a different plant each day
questions.explanation: Only the independent variable (light) should change; everything else is controlled.
questions.hint: Change one thing only.

questions.q: A circuit has a cell, an open switch and a bulb in series. The bulb is off. Why?
options: The open switch breaks the loop so no current flows
questions.explanation: An open switch leaves a gap, breaking the circuit so no current flows.
questions.hint: What does an open switch do to the loop?

questions.q: Two students measure the same length: Student A gets 5.0, 5.1, 4.9 cm; Student B gets 4.0, 6.0, 5.0 cm. True value is 5.0 cm. Which statement is correct?
options: A is more precise; both have the same mean
B is more precise than A
A is less accurate than B
questions.explanation: A's results are close together (precise) and both means are ~5.0 (accurate). B's are spread out (less precise).
questions.hint: Look at the spread of each set.

questions.q: Roughly how long is a human pregnancy (gestation)?
questions.explanation: Human gestation is about 9 months.
questions.hint: About three-quarters of a year.

questions.q: Which is a mixture?
questions.explanation: Air is a mixture of gases not chemically joined.
questions.hint: Which is not chemically bonded?

questions.q: A camel stores fat in its hump. The main benefit is that…
options: It is an energy store for when food is scarce
questions.explanation: The fat is an energy (and water) reserve for the harsh desert where food is rare.
questions.hint: Fat is a store of what?

questions.q: Why can a plant cell keep its shape when placed in water, but an animal cell may burst?
options: The strong cellulose cell wall stops the plant cell bursting
questions.explanation: The rigid cell wall resists the pressure as water enters, while an animal cell has only a membrane and can burst.
questions.hint: What extra strong layer do plant cells have?

questions.q: A student measures the hand spans of 100 pupils and gets a smooth range of values. This is an example of…
options: Discontinuous variation, shown on a bar chart
Continuous variation, shown on a histogram/line graph
questions.explanation: A continuous range of values is continuous variation, displayed as a histogram.
questions.hint: Smooth range = which type, and which graph?

questions.q: In which state are the particles far apart and moving quickly?
questions.explanation: Gas particles are far apart and move fast in all directions.
questions.hint: Which one fills its container?

questions.q: Sound travels fastest in…
questions.explanation: Particles are closest in a solid, so vibrations pass on fastest.
questions.hint: Where are particles closest together?

questions.q: Why does a hot air balloon rise?
options: Heated air expands and becomes less dense than the cooler air around it
questions.explanation: Heating spreads the particles out, making the air less dense so it floats upward.
questions.hint: Heating changes the density of the air.

questions.q: Roughly what is the human hearing range?
questions.explanation: Humans hear from about 20 Hz to 20,000 Hz.
questions.hint: Tens to tens-of-thousands of Hz.

questions.q: Which statement about evaporation and boiling is correct?
options: Both happen only at the surface
Boiling happens throughout the liquid at the boiling point; evaporation happens at the surface at any temperature
Evaporation needs a higher temperature than boiling
questions.explanation: Boiling occurs throughout at the boiling point; evaporation is a surface effect at any temperature.
questions.hint: Where does each one happen in the liquid?

questions.q: What does an ammeter measure?
questions.explanation: An ammeter measures current in amps.
questions.hint: Amps — the clue is in the name.

questions.q: During the menstrual cycle, a period (bleeding) happens when…
options: No fertilised egg implants, so the uterus lining breaks down
questions.explanation: If no fertilised egg implants, the built-up uterus lining is shed as a period.
questions.hint: What happens to the lining if there's no pregnancy?

questions.q: Which structure gives a plant cell its shape and support?
questions.explanation: The cellulose cell wall provides support and shape.
questions.hint: Made of cellulose.

questions.q: Which sequence correctly describes the digestive system level of organisation?
options: Cell and organ are the same
questions.explanation: Cells form muscle tissue, which (with other tissues) forms the stomach (an organ), part of the digestive system.
questions.hint: Build it up from cells.

questions.q: Which is a compound?
questions.explanation: Water is two elements (hydrogen and oxygen) chemically joined — a compound.
questions.hint: Which has more than one type of atom bonded?

questions.q: What is the solid left in the filter paper called?
questions.explanation: The residue is the solid trapped by the filter paper.
questions.hint: It 'resides' in the paper.

questions.q: Higher frequency means a…
questions.explanation: Higher frequency = higher pitch.
questions.hint: Frequency links to pitch.

questions.q: 'Accurate' means a result is…
questions.explanation: Accurate = close to the true/real value.
questions.hint: Bullseye.

questions.q: A substance that can dissolve is described as…
questions.explanation: Soluble means it can dissolve.
questions.hint: Sol-uble.

questions.q: Why does adding a second cell in series make a bulb brighter, in terms of energy?
options: It adds more current that gets used up
It increases the total voltage, transferring more energy to the bulb each second
questions.explanation: Two cells provide more voltage, so more energy is transferred to the bulb per second, making it brighter.
questions.hint: More cells = more voltage = more energy transferred.

questions.q: Gas → liquid is called…
questions.explanation: Gas to liquid is condensing.
questions.hint: Like steam on a cold window.

questions.q: Which correctly orders these from smallest to largest?
questions.explanation: One species (population) → all species (community) → community + surroundings (ecosystem).
questions.hint: Start with one species.

questions.q: Which part is found ONLY in plant cells?
questions.explanation: Chloroplasts (for photosynthesis) are only in plant cells.
questions.hint: Which one is green and makes food?

questions.q: Metals are found where on the periodic table?
questions.explanation: Metals are on the left and middle; non-metals on the right.
questions.hint: Non-metals are on the right.

questions.q: A red blood cell has no nucleus so that it can…
questions.explanation: No nucleus leaves more room for haemoglobin to carry oxygen.
questions.hint: More space inside for what gas?

questions.q: Why do scientists repeat readings and calculate a mean?
options: To reduce the effect of random error and spot anomalies
questions.explanation: Repeats reduce random error and help identify anomalous results.
questions.hint: What kind of error do repeats help with?

questions.q: Which technique separates the colours in black ink?
questions.explanation: Chromatography separates coloured dyes.
questions.hint: Used for inks and dyes.

questions.q: Which method separates sand from water?
questions.explanation: Sand is insoluble, so filtration removes it.
questions.hint: Sand does not dissolve.

questions.q: In a parallel circuit the main current is 0.6 A and splits equally between two identical branches. Current in each branch?
questions.explanation: Current splits between branches: 0.6 A ÷ 2 = 0.3 A each.
questions.hint: It splits between the branches.

questions.q: Energy can be…
questions.explanation: Energy is conserved — it is transferred, not created or destroyed.
questions.hint: Conservation of energy.

questions.q: Liquid → solid is called…
questions.explanation: Liquid to solid is freezing.
questions.hint: Like water turning to ice.

questions.q: What does the nucleus do?
options: Controls the cell and holds DNA
questions.explanation: The nucleus controls the cell's activities and contains genetic information (DNA).
questions.hint: It's the control centre.

questions.q: Why does a mixture not have a fixed melting point, while a pure substance does?
options: The different substances in a mixture melt at different temperatures, giving a range
questions.explanation: Each component melts at its own temperature, so a mixture melts over a range; a pure substance has one fixed point.
questions.hint: Think about each component melting separately.

questions.q: Which Bunsen burner flame is the hot flame used for heating?
questions.explanation: Blue roaring flame (air hole open) is hot; yellow is the cool safety flame.
questions.hint: Open the air hole to get it hotter.

questions.q: What is a producer?
options: A plant that makes its own food
questions.explanation: Producers are plants that make food by photosynthesis.
questions.hint: It makes ('produces') its own food.

questions.q: In chromatography, why must the ink spot be above the solvent level?
options: So the ink does not dissolve straight into the solvent
questions.explanation: If the spot is below the solvent it washes off into the solvent instead of separating up the paper.
questions.hint: What happens if the spot sits in the liquid?

questions.q: Which list contains only elements?
questions.explanation: Oxygen, iron and carbon are each made of only one type of atom — all elements.
questions.hint: Which contains no compounds or mixtures?

questions.q: Two oscilloscope traces show the same note from two instruments. Trace X has taller waves than trace Y, but the waves are equally spaced. What can you conclude?
options: X is higher pitched than Y
X is louder than Y but the same pitch
questions.explanation: Same spacing = same frequency = same pitch; taller waves = larger amplitude = louder. So X is louder at the same pitch.
questions.hint: Equal spacing means equal pitch; height means loudness.

questions.q: The unit of frequency is the…
questions.explanation: Frequency is measured in hertz (Hz).
questions.hint: Hz.

questions.q: In salt water, what is the solute?
questions.explanation: Salt is the solid that dissolves — the solute.
questions.hint: Which part dissolves?

questions.q: In a food chain, the predator is the animal that…
questions.explanation: A predator hunts and eats other animals (its prey).
questions.hint: Who does the hunting?

questions.q: What is the job of the amniotic fluid?
questions.explanation: Amniotic fluid surrounds and protects the baby from bumps.
questions.hint: Think of it as a protective cushion.

questions.q: Why do scientists use several quadrats placed randomly rather than one quadrat?
options: To get a representative average and avoid bias
questions.explanation: Several random samples give a fairer, more representative estimate of the whole area.
questions.hint: One sample might be unusual.

questions.q: A pyramid of numbers for oak tree → caterpillars → birds is an odd shape (narrow at the bottom). Why?
options: One huge tree supports many caterpillars, so the producer level is narrow
questions.explanation: A single large producer (the tree) feeds many small primary consumers, so a number pyramid can be inverted at the base.
questions.hint: How many caterpillars can one tree feed?

questions.q: A vertical column of the periodic table is called a…
questions.explanation: A vertical column is a group; elements in it have similar properties.
questions.hint: Columns go down.

questions.q: Asexual reproduction produces offspring that are…
questions.explanation: Asexual reproduction has one parent and produces identical clones.
questions.hint: One parent, no mixing of genes.

questions.q: A drop of food colouring spreads through still water without stirring. This is because…
options: Particles move randomly, so the colour diffuses
The colour is heavier than water
questions.explanation: Random particle movement causes the dye to diffuse through the water.
questions.hint: Diffusion needs no stirring.

questions.q: Two notes are played. Note A has a higher frequency than note B. Note A is…
questions.explanation: Higher frequency means higher pitch.
questions.hint: Frequency → pitch.

questions.q: Which feature is caused by BOTH genes and environment?
questions.explanation: Height is affected by inherited genes and by diet/environment.
questions.hint: Which one can change with diet but is also inherited?

questions.q: On a heating graph, the temperature stays flat while a solid melts. Why?
options: The energy is used to break forces between particles, not raise temperature
questions.explanation: During melting the supplied energy breaks the forces holding particles, so temperature stays constant.
questions.hint: Where is the energy going during melting?

mocks.title: Paper 1 — Mock 7

questions.q: A voltmeter is connected…
questions.explanation: A voltmeter is connected in parallel across the component.

questions.q: The liquid that passes through the filter paper is the…
questions.explanation: The filtrate is the liquid that drips through.

questions.q: Both animal and plant cells contain which set of parts?

questions.q: Pollination is the transfer of pollen from the anther to the…
questions.explanation: Pollination moves pollen from the anther to the stigma.
questions.hint: Where does pollen need to land?

questions.explanation: Several random samples give a fairer, more representative estimate of the whole area.
questions.hint: One sample might be unusual.

questions.q: A student gets 4.0, 4.1, 4.0, 7.8, 4.1 g. Which is the anomalous result and what should they do?

questions.q: Sound travels fastest in…

questions.q: Liquid → solid is called…

questions.q: Which word means 'all the rabbits living in one field'?
questions.explanation: A population is all the members of one species in an area.

questions.q: Asexual reproduction produces offspring that are…
questions.explanation: Asexual reproduction has one parent and produces identical clones.

questions.q: Which is a mixture?
questions.explanation: Sea water contains water plus many dissolved salts — a mixture.

questions.q: A vertical column of the periodic table is called a…
questions.explanation: A vertical column is a group; elements in it have similar properties.

questions.q: A change of state such as melting is best described as…

A physical change that can be reversed

questions.q: Two notes are played. Note A has a higher frequency than note B. Note A is…

questions.explanation: Current is the same everywhere in a series circuit, so it is still 0.3 A.

questions.q: Which is NOT one of the seven life processes?
questions.explanation: Photosynthesis is how plants make food, but it is not one of the 7 MRS GREN processes (that's Nutrition).

questions.q: A food web is…
questions.explanation: A food web links several food chains to show multiple feeding relationships.
questions.hint: More than one chain.

questions.q: What is the 'range' of these values: 3, 5, 8, 11 N?
questions.explanation: Range is the smallest to the largest value: 3–11 N.

questions.q: Temperature measured in °C is an example of what type of data?

questions.q: What is the chemical symbol for sodium?
questions.explanation: Sodium's symbol is Na.
questions.hint: It comes from its Latin name natrium.

questions.q: How many atoms in total are in one molecule of H₂SO₄?

questions.q: Which list contains ONLY pure substances?
questions.explanation: Distilled water, pure copper and oxygen each contain one substance only.
questions.hint: Pure = single substance, no mixtures.

questions.q: What does an ammeter measure?
questions.explanation: An ammeter measures current in amps.
questions.hint: Amps — the clue is in the name.

questions.q: Higher frequency means a…
questions.explanation: Higher frequency = higher pitch.
questions.hint: Frequency links to pitch.

questions.q: Why does a substance often expand only a little when it melts, but expand a great deal when it boils?
options: Going to a gas spreads particles far apart, hugely increasing volume
questions.explanation: Solid and liquid particles both touch, so melting changes spacing little; becoming a gas spreads particles far apart, so volume increases enormously.
questions.hint: Compare the spacing change for melt vs boil.

questions.q: An echo is…
options: Sound reflected off a hard surface
questions.explanation: An echo is reflected sound.
questions.hint: Sound bouncing back.

questions.q: Complete the word equation: magnesium + oxygen → …
questions.explanation: Magnesium reacts with oxygen to form magnesium oxide.
questions.hint: Metal + oxygen → metal oxide.

questions.q: Which separation technique would best separate two liquids that mix together but have different boiling points?
questions.explanation: Fractional distillation separates miscible liquids by their different boiling points.
questions.hint: Liquids + different boiling points.

questions.q: In a parallel circuit the main current is 0.6 A and splits equally between two identical branches. Current in each branch?
questions.explanation: Current splits between branches: 0.6 A ÷ 2 = 0.3 A each.
questions.hint: It splits between the branches.

questions.q: Why might an older person not hear a very high-pitched whistle that a child can hear?
options: We lose the ability to hear high frequencies as we age
questions.explanation: Hearing of high frequencies declines with age, so the top of the range is lost.
questions.hint: Which end of the hearing range fades with age?

questions.q: What is the solid left in the filter paper called?
questions.explanation: The residue is the solid trapped by the filter paper.
questions.hint: It 'resides' in the paper.

questions.q: A balance reads 2 g when nothing is on it. This is an example of…
questions.explanation: Equipment not reading zero when empty is a zero error.
questions.hint: What should it read when empty?

questions.q: Why does adding a second cell in series make a bulb brighter, in terms of energy?
options: It adds more current that gets used up
It increases the total voltage, transferring more energy to the bulb each second
questions.explanation: Two cells provide more voltage, so more energy is transferred to the bulb per second, making it brighter.
questions.hint: More cells = more voltage = more energy transferred.

questions.q: Which is an example of inherited variation?
questions.explanation: Natural eye colour is determined by genes, so it is inherited.
questions.hint: Which one were you born with?

questions.q: Fertilisation is the joining of…
options: A sperm nucleus and an egg nucleus
questions.explanation: Fertilisation joins a sperm nucleus with an egg nucleus to form a zygote.
questions.hint: One from each parent.

questions.q: What is diffusion?
options: Particles spreading from high to low concentration
questions.explanation: Diffusion is the spreading of particles from high to low concentration.
questions.hint: Like a smell spreading across a room.

questions.q: During the menstrual cycle, a period (bleeding) happens when…
options: No fertilised egg implants, so the uterus lining breaks down
questions.explanation: If no fertilised egg implants, the built-up uterus lining is shed as a period.
questions.hint: What happens to the lining if there's no pregnancy?

mocks.title: Paper 1 — Mock 8
mocks.instructions: 60 multiple-choice questions. Answer all. 45 minutes. 1 mark each.

questions.q: Where does a baby develop?
questions.explanation: The baby develops in the uterus (womb).
questions.hint: Also called the womb.

questions.q: The liquid that passes through the filter paper is the…
questions.explanation: The filtrate is the liquid that drips through.
questions.hint: It 'filters' through.

questions.q: Solid → liquid is called…
questions.explanation: Solid to liquid is melting.
questions.hint: Like ice turning to water.

questions.q: Ultrasound is sound that is…
questions.explanation: Ultrasound has a frequency above the human hearing range (20,000 Hz).
questions.hint: 'Ultra' = beyond the top of our range.

questions.q: Two notes are played. Note A has a higher frequency than note B. Note A is…
questions.explanation: Higher frequency means higher pitch.
questions.hint: Frequency → pitch.

questions.q: Which variable do you change in an experiment?
questions.explanation: The independent variable is the one you deliberately change. It goes on the x-axis.
questions.hint: It's the cause, not the effect — think 'I change it'.

questions.q: Gas → liquid is called…
questions.explanation: Gas to liquid is condensing.
questions.hint: Like steam on a cold window.

questions.q: Roughly how much energy passes to the next level of a food chain?
questions.explanation: Only about 10% passes on; the rest is lost as heat, movement and waste.
questions.hint: It's a small fraction.

questions.q: Which feature is caused by BOTH genes and environment?
questions.explanation: Height is affected by inherited genes and by diet/environment.
questions.hint: Which one can change with diet but is also inherited?

questions.q: How many atoms are in one molecule of CO₂?
questions.explanation: 1 carbon + 2 oxygen = 3 atoms.
questions.hint: Count the carbon and the oxygens.

questions.q: Which flower part makes pollen?
questions.explanation: The anther (part of the stamen) makes pollen.
questions.hint: It's part of the male stamen.

questions.q: A pure substance melts at exactly 0°C. A sample melts from -3°C to 2°C. What does this tell you?
questions.explanation: Melting over a range (not a sharp point) shows it is impure / a mixture.
questions.hint: Pure = sharp fixed point.

questions.q: An animal that eats the producer is called a…
questions.explanation: The first consumer (herbivore) is the primary consumer.
questions.hint: First in line after the plant.

questions.q: Why does adding a second cell in series make a bulb brighter, in terms of energy?
options: It adds more current that gets used up
It increases the total voltage, transferring more energy to the bulb each second
questions.explanation: Two cells provide more voltage, so more energy is transferred to the bulb per second, making it brighter.
questions.hint: More cells = more voltage = more energy transferred.

questions.q: In a parallel circuit, if one branch bulb breaks, the others…
questions.explanation: Other branches still have a complete loop, so they stay on.
questions.hint: Each branch is separate.

questions.q: A microscope eyepiece is ×10 and objective ×20. Total magnification?
questions.hint: Multiply the two lenses.

questions.q: Why do elements in the same group of the periodic table behave similarly?
options: They have similar properties because of their position in the same column
questions.explanation: Elements in a group (column) share similar chemical properties.
questions.hint: Groups are the columns with shared behaviour.

questions.q: Two ammeters in a series circuit read different values (0.4 A and 0.2 A). What is the most likely explanation?
options: There is a measurement/fault error — series current must be equal
questions.explanation: Current is identical everywhere in series, so unequal readings indicate a faulty meter or wrong connection.
questions.hint: What should series current readings be?

questions.q: Which part is found ONLY in plant cells?
questions.explanation: Chloroplasts (for photosynthesis) are only in plant cells.
questions.hint: Which one is green and makes food?

questions.q: What is the 'range' of these values: 3, 5, 8, 11 N?
questions.explanation: Range is the smallest to the largest value: 3–11 N.
questions.hint: Smallest and biggest.

questions.q: On an oscilloscope, a high-pitched note shows…
questions.explanation: High pitch = high frequency = waves bunched closer together.
questions.hint: Pitch changes how close the waves are.

questions.q: Why does a substance often expand only a little when it melts, but expand a great deal when it boils?
options: Going to a gas spreads particles far apart, hugely increasing volume
questions.explanation: Solid and liquid particles both touch, so melting changes spacing little; becoming a gas spreads particles far apart, so volume increases enormously.
questions.hint: Compare the spacing change for melt vs boil.

questions.q: Which is a renewable energy resource?
questions.explanation: Wind is renewable — it won't run out.
questions.hint: Which one keeps being replaced naturally?

questions.q: The formula CaCO₃ contains how many oxygen atoms?
questions.explanation: The small 3 applies to the oxygen, giving 3 oxygen atoms.
questions.hint: Look at the subscript after the O.

questions.q: Results are very close together but all far from the true value. They are…
questions.explanation: Close together = precise; far from true value = not accurate.
questions.hint: Tightly grouped arrows, but off the bullseye.

questions.q: A camel stores fat in its hump. The main benefit is that…
options: It is an energy store for when food is scarce
questions.explanation: The fat is an energy (and water) reserve for the harsh desert where food is rare.
questions.hint: Fat is a store of what?

questions.q: Liquid → solid is called…
questions.explanation: Liquid to solid is freezing.
questions.hint: Like water turning to ice.

questions.q: Which change would make a guitar string produce a higher-pitched note?
questions.explanation: Higher frequency (faster vibration) raises the pitch; plucking harder only changes loudness.
questions.hint: Pitch depends on frequency, not how hard you pluck.

questions.q: Why is it more energy-efficient for humans to eat plants directly than to eat animals that ate the plants?
options: Each step up a food chain loses ~90% of the energy, so eating lower down wastes less
questions.explanation: Eating at a lower trophic level avoids the large energy losses between levels.
questions.hint: Energy is lost at each level you go up.

questions.q: Metals are found where on the periodic table?
questions.explanation: Metals are on the left and middle; non-metals on the right.
questions.hint: Non-metals are on the right.

questions.q: An element is a substance made of…
questions.explanation: An element contains only one type of atom.
questions.hint: One type of atom only.

questions.q: A food web is…
questions.explanation: A food web links several food chains to show multiple feeding relationships.
questions.hint: More than one chain.

questions.q: Why might an older person not hear a very high-pitched whistle that a child can hear?
options: We lose the ability to hear high frequencies as we age
questions.explanation: Hearing of high frequencies declines with age, so the top of the range is lost.
questions.hint: Which end of the hearing range fades with age?

questions.q: Why does a hot air balloon rise?
options: Heated air expands and becomes less dense than the cooler air around it
questions.explanation: Heating spreads the particles out, making the air less dense so it floats upward.
questions.hint: Heating changes the density of the air.

questions.q: Pollination is the transfer of pollen from the anther to the…
questions.explanation: Pollination moves pollen from the anther to the stigma.
questions.hint: Where does pollen need to land?

questions.q: What does an ammeter measure?
questions.explanation: An ammeter measures current in amps.
questions.hint: Amps — the clue is in the name.

questions.q: Where should units go in a results table?
questions.explanation: Units go in the heading, e.g. Time (s). Numbers in the cells have no units.
questions.hint: Think about how a tidy table looks.

questions.q: Which energy transfer happens in a torch (flashlight)?
questions.explanation: Chemical store in the battery transfers electrically to the bulb, then to light (and wasted heat).
questions.hint: Start with the battery's store.

questions.q: Complete the word equation: magnesium + oxygen → …
questions.explanation: Magnesium reacts with oxygen to form magnesium oxide.
questions.hint: Metal + oxygen → metal oxide.

questions.q: What does the nucleus do?
options: Controls the cell and holds DNA
questions.explanation: The nucleus controls the cell's activities and contains genetic information (DNA).
questions.hint: It's the control centre.

questions.q: Which statement about the nucleus is correct?
options: It contains DNA that controls the cell
It is only in plant cells
questions.explanation: The nucleus holds DNA and controls cell activities; it is in both animal and plant cells.
questions.hint: Control centre with genetic info.

questions.q: Where are sperm cells made?
questions.explanation: Sperm are made in the testes.
questions.hint: The male organ that makes sperm.

questions.q: Put these in order from smallest to largest.
questions.explanation: Cell → Tissue → Organ → Organ system → Organism.
questions.hint: Start with the building block.

questions.q: Which word means 'all the rabbits living in one field'?
questions.explanation: A population is all the members of one species in an area.
questions.hint: One species only.

questions.q: Two dyes are tested by chromatography. Dye A travels 8 cm and dye B travels 3 cm in the same time. Which is more soluble?
questions.explanation: More soluble dyes are carried further up the paper, so dye A is more soluble.
questions.hint: Which moved further?

questions.q: Why do you see lightning before you hear thunder?
options: Light travels much faster than sound
questions.explanation: Light travels almost instantly; sound is much slower, so it arrives later.
questions.hint: Compare the speeds of light and sound.

questions.q: What is a producer?
options: A plant that makes its own food
questions.explanation: Producers are plants that make food by photosynthesis.
questions.hint: It makes ('produces') its own food.

questions.q: In a series circuit, the current at point A (before a bulb) is 0.3 A. What is the current after the bulb?
questions.explanation: Current is the same everywhere in a series circuit, so it is still 0.3 A.
questions.hint: Series = same current everywhere.

questions.q: Which technique separates the colours in black ink?
questions.explanation: Chromatography separates coloured dyes.
questions.hint: Used for inks and dyes.

questions.q: In salt water, what is the solute?
questions.explanation: Salt is the solid that dissolves — the solute.
questions.hint: Which part dissolves?

questions.q: On a heating graph, the temperature stays flat while a solid melts. Why?
options: The energy is used to break forces between particles, not raise temperature
questions.explanation: During melting the supplied energy breaks the forces holding particles, so temperature stays constant.
questions.hint: Where is the energy going during melting?

questions.q: Which graph should you draw for two sets of continuous data?
questions.explanation: Two continuous variables → line graph with a line of best fit.
questions.hint: Bars are for categories.

questions.q: Which is NOT one of the seven life processes?
questions.explanation: Photosynthesis is how plants make food, but it is not one of the 7 MRS GREN processes (that's Nutrition).
questions.hint: Think MRS GREN.

questions.q: A student gets 4.0, 4.1, 4.0, 7.8, 4.1 g. Which is the anomalous result and what should they do?
options: 7.8 g — ignore it when calculating the mean
questions.explanation: 7.8 g doesn't fit the pattern; ignore it before averaging the rest.
questions.hint: Which value doesn't fit the others?

questions.q: Which set-up gives the most valid (fair) test of how light affects plant growth?
options: Different plants, different pots, different water
Same plant type, same water and soil, only light changed
Measure a different plant each day
questions.explanation: Only the independent variable (light) should change; everything else is controlled.
questions.hint: Change one thing only.

questions.q: Why does a mixture not have a fixed melting point, while a pure substance does?
options: The different substances in a mixture melt at different temperatures, giving a range
questions.explanation: Each component melts at its own temperature, so a mixture melts over a range; a pure substance has one fixed point.
questions.hint: Think about each component melting separately.

questions.q: A drop of food colouring spreads through still water without stirring. This is because…
options: Particles move randomly, so the colour diffuses
The colour is heavier than water
questions.explanation: Random particle movement causes the dye to diffuse through the water.
questions.hint: Diffusion needs no stirring.

questions.q: Why does a muscle cell contain many mitochondria?
options: To release lots of energy for contraction
questions.explanation: Contracting needs energy, supplied by respiration in mitochondria.
questions.hint: What do muscles need to move?

questions.q: What is the job of the amniotic fluid?
questions.explanation: Amniotic fluid surrounds and protects the baby from bumps.
questions.hint: Think of it as a protective cushion.

questions.q: Which method gets pure water FROM salt water?
questions.explanation: Distillation evaporates the water and condenses it back, leaving salt behind.
questions.hint: You want to keep the liquid, not the solid.

mocks.title: Paper 1 — Mock 9
mocks.instructions: 60 multiple-choice questions. Fresh question set (different from Mocks 1-8). 45 minutes. 1 mark each.

questions.q: Which of the following is a female reproductive organ?
questions.explanation: The ovary is a female reproductive organ that produces eggs. The other options are all male reproductive structures.
questions.hint: Think about where eggs are made in females.

questions.q: Which separation technique would be most suitable for separating a mixture of two immiscible liquids with similar boiling points?
questions.explanation: A separating funnel is used to separate immiscible liquids (liquids that do not mix) regardless of their boiling points.
questions.hint: Immiscible means the liquids don't mix - think of oil and water.

questions.q: Which statement best explains why specialised cells have many mitochondria?
options: They need more mitochondria to store food
They perform functions that require large amounts of energy
Mitochondria protect the cell from damage
Multiple mitochondria help cells divide faster
questions.explanation: Cells with high energy demands, like muscle cells and nerve cells, contain many mitochondria to produce sufficient ATP through aerobic respiration.
questions.hint: Think about which body cells work the hardest and need the most energy.

questions.q: In paper chromatography, a dye travels 6 cm while the solvent travels 10 cm. What is the Rf value?
questions.explanation: Rf = distance travelled by substance ÷ distance travelled by solvent = 6 ÷ 10 = 0.6
questions.hint: Rf value = divide the distances

questions.q: What is a hypothesis?
options: A final conclusion after an experiment
A testable prediction based on scientific knowledge
A list of equipment needed for an experiment
The method you use to collect data
questions.explanation: A hypothesis is an educated guess or prediction that can be tested through experimentation. It is based on existing scientific knowledge.
questions.hint: It's a prediction that you can test — think 'I think... because...'.

questions.q: A student builds a parallel circuit with two identical resistors. How does the total resistance compare to a series circuit with the same two resistors?
options: Both circuits have the same resistance
It depends on the battery voltage
questions.explanation: In parallel circuits, current has multiple paths, so total resistance is lower than in series. For identical resistors, parallel resistance is half the series resistance.
questions.hint: More paths for current = easier flow = lower resistance.

questions.q: What does the wavelength of a wave measure?
options: The time taken for one wave
The distance from one wave crest to the next crest
questions.explanation: Wavelength is the distance between two identical points on a wave, such as from one crest to the next crest.
questions.hint: Look at the distance between the highest points of a wave.

questions.q: An atom of nitrogen has 7 protons and 7 neutrons. What is its mass number?
questions.explanation: Mass number = protons + neutrons = 7 + 7 = 14
questions.hint: Mass number counts both protons and neutrons in the nucleus.

questions.q: Which of the following is an example of a predator-prey relationship?
options: A remora fish and a shark
questions.explanation: A predator hunts and eats prey. Lions hunt zebras. Bees and flowers have a mutualistic relationship (both benefit). Remora fish benefit from the shark but the shark is unaffected (commensalism). Clownfish and anemones both benefit (mutualism).
questions.hint: A predator kills and eats another animal. Which pair shows one hunting the other?

questions.q: Which type of data is 'colour of a flower'?
questions.explanation: Categoric data consists of words or labels, like colours, names, or types. It cannot be measured numerically.
questions.hint: Is this a word/label or a number you can measure?

questions.q: In the food chain: lettuce → caterpillar → bird → cat, what is the cat?
questions.explanation: The cat is at the top of this chain, eating the bird (secondary consumer), making it a tertiary consumer.
questions.hint: Count how many steps up from the producer the cat is.

questions.q: Liquid → gas is called…
questions.explanation: Liquid to gas is evaporating, like water turning into steam when heated.
questions.hint: What happens to puddles on a sunny day?

questions.q: Which statement about renewable and non-renewable energy sources is correct?
options: Renewable sources can run out eventually
Solar energy is renewed naturally by the Sun
questions.explanation: Solar energy is renewable because the Sun continuously produces energy and will do so for billions of years. The other statements are false.
questions.hint: 'Renewable' means it is naturally replaced or will last very long.

questions.q: Why is the placenta an important organ during pregnancy?
options: It is where the baby's heart develops
It allows nutrient and oxygen exchange between mother and baby
It controls how fast the baby grows
questions.explanation: The placenta is embedded in the uterus wall and allows oxygen and nutrients to pass from mother's blood to baby's blood, and waste products to pass back.
questions.hint: What structure needs to pass materials between two separate blood systems?

questions.q: Which of the following is the correct order of organisation from smallest to largest?
questions.explanation: Cells are the smallest living units. Similar cells form tissues, tissues form organs, and organs work together in organ systems.
questions.hint: Start with the smallest unit of life and build up.

questions.q: What is the chemical symbol for gold?
questions.explanation: Gold's symbol is Au, which comes from its Latin name 'aurum'.
questions.hint: The symbol comes from an old language name for gold.

questions.q: A substance has a melting point range of 45°C to 52°C. What does this suggest?
options: It is a gas at room temperature
questions.explanation: A wide melting point range indicates the substance is impure. Pure substances have a sharp, fixed melting point.
questions.hint: Compare to a pure substance which melts at one exact temperature.

questions.q: When light travels from air into glass, it slows down and bends. This is an example of…
questions.explanation: Refraction is the bending of a wave when it enters a different medium and changes speed. Diffraction is spreading; reflection is bouncing back.
questions.hint: What happens to a wave when it enters a different medium and changes speed?

questions.q: In which state of matter are the particles closest together?
options: All states have particles equally spaced
questions.explanation: Solid particles are closest together because they are tightly packed in fixed positions.
questions.hint: Which state takes up the least amount of space?

questions.q: In plant cells, the vacuole is mainly used for:
options: Storing water and maintaining cell shape
questions.explanation: The large central vacuole in plant cells stores water, nutrients, and helps the plant cell maintain its rigid shape and firmness.
questions.hint: It keeps the plant cell firm and stores cell sap.

questions.q: What is the correct order of state changes from solid to gas?
questions.explanation: A solid first melts to become a liquid, then the liquid evaporates to become a gas.
questions.hint: Think about what happens to ice when it warms up.

questions.q: Which of the following is an example of discrete data?
options: The height of a student in cm
The number of leaves on a plant
The mass of a rock in grams
questions.explanation: Discrete data can only have whole number values - you cannot have 3.7 leaves. The other options are continuous (can have decimal values).
questions.hint: Think about whether you could have a decimal/fraction of the measurement.

questions.q: A thermometer reads 23°C, 23°C, 23°C, and 45°C. What is the most likely explanation for the 45°C reading?
options: It is an anomalous result caused by random error
Thermometers always give one incorrect reading
questions.explanation: The 45°C reading is anomalous—it doesn't fit the pattern of the other three readings and should be ignored when calculating the mean.
questions.hint: Which reading is very different from the others?

questions.q: Which renewable energy source is most reliable because it works day and night?
questions.explanation: Geothermal power uses heat from inside the Earth, which is constant day and night. Solar only works during daylight and wind varies throughout the day.
questions.hint: Which energy source does not depend on weather or time of day?

questions.q: Which of these is an example of inherited variation?
questions.explanation: Eye colour is inherited from your parents through genes, making it inherited variation.
questions.hint: This characteristic comes from your parents' genes.

questions.q: Which of the following is a transverse wave?
options: A wave on a stretched string
A compression wave in a spring
questions.explanation: A wave on a stretched string oscillates perpendicular (at right angles) to the direction of wave travel, making it transverse. Sound waves are longitudinal (parallel to travel direction).
questions.hint: Transverse waves vibrate perpendicular to the direction the wave travels.

questions.q: Which of the following is a conductor?
questions.explanation: Aluminium is a metal that allows electricity to flow through it easily — it is a conductor.
questions.hint: Conductors are usually shiny metals.

questions.q: Which of the following correctly describes the amplitude of a wave?
options: The number of waves per second
The distance the wave travels in one second
The maximum distance a particle moves from its resting position
The distance from one crest to the next trough
questions.explanation: Amplitude is how far a particle moves away from its resting position, measured from the centre to the crest or trough.
questions.hint: Think about how far up and down a particle moves from the middle.

questions.q: Which organelle is responsible for releasing energy in both plant and animal cells through aerobic respiration?
questions.explanation: Mitochondria are the powerhouses of the cell, breaking down glucose to release energy in the form of ATP during aerobic respiration.
questions.hint: Think about which organelle produces the energy currency of the cell.

questions.q: Which of these is a mixture?
questions.explanation: Salt water contains salt and water mixed together — it is a mixture.
questions.hint: A mixture has two or more substances that are not chemically joined.

questions.q: What is the name of the solid left behind when sea water is evaporated?
questions.explanation: When sea water evaporates, salt crystallises and is left as a solid residue.
questions.hint: What is the main dissolved substance in sea water?

questions.q: When sodium and chlorine react to form sodium chloride, electrons are transferred from sodium to chlorine. What type of bonding occurs in the product?
questions.explanation: When electrons are transferred from one atom to another, ions are formed. The electrostatic attraction between these oppositely charged ions is called ionic bonding.
questions.hint: What happens when electrons move from one atom to another?

questions.q: Why do solids have a definite shape?
options: Particles are in fixed positions and held by strong forces
questions.explanation: Solids have a definite shape because their particles are held in fixed positions by strong attractive forces between them.
questions.hint: What keeps solid particles from moving around?

questions.q: The stomach is an organ made of several different tissues. Which tissues are found in the stomach wall?
options: Muscle tissue, epithelial tissue, and connective tissue
questions.explanation: The stomach wall contains muscle tissue (for churning), epithelial tissue (lining the stomach), and connective tissue (holding layers together).
questions.hint: An organ needs to move, protect, and support itself.

questions.q: Two identical bulbs are connected in series to a battery. If one bulb fails and breaks the circuit, what happens to the other bulb?
questions.explanation: In a series circuit, if one component breaks, the circuit is broken and current cannot flow anywhere. Both bulbs go off.
questions.hint: In series, there is only one path for current to follow.

questions.q: Which of the following is a property of sound waves?
options: They can travel through a vacuum
They travel faster in air than in water
They do not require a medium to travel
questions.explanation: Sound waves are longitudinal waves where particles vibrate parallel to the direction of energy flow. They cannot travel through a vacuum and travel faster in denser media like water.
questions.hint: Think about whether sound needs something to travel through.

questions.q: What is a food web?
options: A simple linear chain of organisms
Many interconnected food chains showing different feeding relationships
questions.explanation: A food web is more complex than a single food chain—it shows multiple food chains linked together, representing the real feeding relationships in an ecosystem.
questions.hint: A web has many connections, while a chain is just one line.

questions.q: Which of the following is a conductor of electricity?
questions.explanation: Aluminum is a metal and conducts electricity well. Rubber, glass and wood are insulators.
questions.hint: Metals are usually good conductors.

questions.q: What is the chemical symbol for oxygen?
questions.explanation: The chemical symbol for oxygen is O (a single capital letter). O₂ is a molecule of oxygen gas with two atoms.
questions.hint: Chemical symbols are usually one or two letters.

questions.q: Why do decomposers occupy a unique position in food webs rather than being part of a linear food chain?
options: They break down dead organic matter from all trophic levels and return nutrients to the soil, feeding on material from multiple levels
They are not important to ecosystems
questions.explanation: Decomposers consume dead organisms and waste from all trophic levels, not just one level above them, so they interact with the entire food web structure.
questions.hint: Where does a decomposer get its food from compared to a consumer?

questions.q: A drum is struck with a drumstick. Which sequence correctly shows the energy transfers?
options: Light energy → Sound energy → Kinetic energy
Kinetic energy → Sound energy → Thermal energy
Kinetic energy → Sound energy → Kinetic energy of air particles
Thermal energy → Kinetic energy → Sound energy
questions.explanation: The drumstick has kinetic energy. When it strikes the drum, it creates sound energy. This sound energy causes air particles to move (kinetic energy of the particles).
questions.hint: Start with the moving drumstick and follow what happens step by step.

questions.q: What is an anomalous result?
options: A result that doesn't follow the pattern
A result that is very large
questions.explanation: An anomalous result is one that doesn't fit the pattern of your other results. It may be due to experimental error or an unexpected event.
questions.hint: It's the odd one out that doesn't fit the pattern.

questions.q: Which of these is an example of competition in an ecosystem?
options: Two rabbits eating grass from the same field
A fungus breaking down a log
questions.explanation: Competition occurs when two organisms need the same resource. Two rabbits competing for the same grass is competition.
questions.hint: Competition is when organisms struggle for the same thing they need.

questions.q: What is the name of the variable you measure or observe as a result of your experiment?
questions.explanation: The dependent variable is what you measure - it is the effect or result that depends on what you changed (the independent variable).
questions.hint: It 'depends on' the independent variable. It is the result you measure.

questions.q: Which of the following shows a correct food chain?
questions.explanation: A food chain must start with a producer (grass) and show the correct feeding direction: grass is eaten by rabbit, and rabbit is eaten by fox.
questions.hint: Remember: arrows point FROM food TO the thing eating it.

questions.q: Which of the following is the correct order of organisation in living organisms, from smallest to largest?
questions.explanation: The correct hierarchy is: cells combine to form tissues, tissues form organs, organs form systems, and systems make up the organism.
questions.hint: Start with the smallest living unit.

questions.q: In a sugar solution, what is the solvent?
questions.explanation: The solvent is the liquid that dissolves the solute. Water dissolves the sugar.
questions.hint: Which part is the liquid?

questions.q: A student observes a cheek cell under a microscope at ×400 magnification. The nucleus appears 0.2 mm in diameter. What is the actual diameter of the nucleus?
questions.explanation: Real size = image size ÷ magnification = 0.2 ÷ 400 = 0.0005 mm.
questions.hint: Remember: real size = image size divided by magnification.

questions.q: Which process produces variation in offspring?
questions.explanation: Sexual reproduction produces variation because genetic material from two different parents combines. Asexual reproduction produces identical offspring.
questions.hint: It involves two parents contributing genes.

questions.q: What does a voltmeter measure?
questions.explanation: A voltmeter measures the potential difference (voltage) across a component in volts.
questions.hint: Volt — it's in the name!

questions.q: Which method would you use to separate dye colours in an ink sample?
questions.explanation: Chromatography separates different coloured dyes by moving them at different speeds through paper.
questions.hint: This method is used to separate colours and pigments.

questions.q: During freezing, what happens to the particles as a liquid cools down?
options: Particles vibrate faster and move closer together
Particles vibrate slower and arrange into a fixed pattern
questions.explanation: As temperature drops during freezing, particles vibrate less. They slow down enough that forces between them hold them in fixed, orderly positions forming a solid.
questions.hint: What happens to particle vibration as temperature decreases?

questions.q: In a forest, a pine tree and a fungus form a mycorrhizal relationship where the fungus grows around the tree's roots. The fungus gets sugars from the tree, and the tree gains better water and mineral absorption. Which organisms benefit from this interaction?
options: Both the tree and the fungus
questions.explanation: Both organisms gain advantages from this relationship. The fungus obtains sugars for energy, while the tree gains improved access to water and minerals. This is mutualism.
questions.hint: Identify what each organism gets from the relationship.

questions.q: What is the smallest particle of an element called?
questions.explanation: An atom is the smallest particle of an element that still has the properties of that element.
questions.hint: Think about what the smallest unit is...

questions.q: What is the name of the cell that is produced when a sperm and egg join together?
questions.explanation: A zygote is the cell formed when a sperm nucleus fuses with an egg nucleus during fertilisation.
questions.hint: It's what forms immediately after fertilisation.

questions.q: Liquid → gas is called…
questions.explanation: Liquid to gas is evaporating, like water turning into steam.
questions.hint: What happens to puddles on a sunny day?

questions.q: Which statement about gas particles is true?
options: They move slowly in all directions
They are far apart and move very quickly
They are arranged in a fixed pattern
questions.explanation: Gas particles are far apart, move very quickly in all directions, and have no fixed arrangement.
questions.hint: What fills a balloon or a room?

questions.q: A student repeats a mass measurement four times and gets: 25.0 g, 25.1 g, 25.0 g, 25.2 g. The actual mass is 35.0 g. Which statement is true?
options: The results are accurate but not precise
The results are precise but not accurate
The results are both accurate and precise
The results are neither accurate nor precise
questions.explanation: Results are close together (25.0–25.2 g) so they are precise. But they are far from 35.0 g so they are not accurate—there is a systematic error.
questions.hint: Check if readings are close to each other AND close to the true value.

questions.q: In the periodic table, why do elements in Group 1 (alkali metals) have similar chemical properties?
options: They have the same number of neutrons
They have the same number of electrons in their outermost shell
They have the same atomic mass
They all form the same compounds
questions.explanation: Elements in the same group have the same number of electrons in their outermost shell, which determines their chemical behavior and reactivity patterns.
questions.hint: What feature of an atom determines how it reacts with other elements?

questions.q: Which statement correctly describes the role of the umbilical cord?
options: It produces hormones to control the baby's growth
It carries blood containing nutrients and oxygen from mother to baby
It protects the baby from infections
It removes only waste products from the baby
questions.explanation: The umbilical cord contains blood vessels that transport oxygen, nutrients, and waste between the mother and developing baby.
questions.hint: Think of it like a pipeline connecting the baby to the mother's blood supply.

mocks.title: Paper 1 — Mock 10
mocks.instructions: 60 multiple-choice questions. Fresh question set (different from Mocks 1-8). 45 minutes. 1 mark each.

questions.q: Which statement correctly describes a pure substance?
options: It contains only one type of particle
questions.explanation: A pure substance contains only one type of particle or molecule throughout.
questions.hint: A pure substance has a fixed composition.

questions.q: What is the main function of the placenta during pregnancy?
options: To exchange nutrients and oxygen between mother and fetus
To protect the baby from infection
To control the baby's heart rate
questions.explanation: The placenta allows the exchange of nutrients, oxygen, and waste between the mother's blood and the fetus's blood, without them mixing directly.
questions.hint: Think about what the baby needs to survive and grow in the womb.

questions.q: A sound wave travels through air at 340 m/s. If the wavelength is 0.68 m, what is the frequency of the sound?
questions.explanation: Using the wave equation v = f × λ, we rearrange to f = v ÷ λ = 340 ÷ 0.68 = 500 Hz.
questions.hint: Use the formula: frequency = speed ÷ wavelength

questions.q: Which of the following is an example of a qualitative observation?
options: The mass decreased from 10 g to 8 g
The volume of gas was 250 mL
questions.explanation: A qualitative observation describes a quality or characteristic (colour, smell, texture) without numbers. The other options are quantitative—they use measurements.
questions.hint: Qualitative = descriptions without numbers. Which doesn't have a number in it?

questions.q: Which statement best explains why an ecosystem needs producers?
options: Producers eat all the waste from consumers
Producers capture energy from the sun and make it available to all other organisms
Producers are the only organisms that need energy
Producers prevent predators from eating herbivores
questions.explanation: Producers (plants) convert sunlight into chemical energy through photosynthesis. This energy enters the ecosystem and flows through all food chains. Without producers, there would be no energy source for any other organism.
questions.hint: Think about where all the energy in an ecosystem comes from.

questions.q: Which organism breaks down dead material and returns nutrients to the soil?
questions.explanation: Decomposers like bacteria and fungi break down dead organisms and recycle nutrients.
questions.hint: These organisms 'decompose' (break down) dead things.

questions.q: Which statement about the periodic table is true?
questions.explanation: The periodic table is an organized list of all known elements arranged by their properties.
questions.hint: Think about what the periodic table organizes.

questions.q: Which of these is an example of inherited variation?
questions.explanation: Eye colour is inherited from your parents through genes, making it inherited variation.
questions.hint: This characteristic comes from your parents' genes.

questions.q: In paper chromatography, a dye travels 6 cm while the solvent travels 10 cm. What is the Rf value?
questions.explanation: Rf = distance travelled by substance ÷ distance travelled by solvent = 6 ÷ 10 = 0.6
questions.hint: Rf value = divide the distances

questions.q: Which process involves particles gaining enough energy to overcome all forces between them and escape into the air?
questions.explanation: Vaporisation (boiling or evaporation) is when particles gain enough energy to completely overcome forces and become a gas.
questions.hint: Which change of state takes a liquid to a gas?

questions.q: A container of gas is cooled while keeping the volume constant. What happens to the pressure?
options: The gas changes state to liquid
questions.explanation: When cooled, gas particles move slower and hit the container walls less often and with less force, so the pressure decreases.
questions.hint: How does the motion of particles change when temperature drops?

questions.q: What does the law of conservation of energy state?
options: Energy cannot be created or destroyed, only changed from one form to another
Energy only exists in moving objects
questions.explanation: Energy cannot be created or destroyed, only converted from one form to another (e.g., chemical to electrical to light).
questions.hint: Think about whether energy disappears or just changes.

questions.q: Which type of data is the colour of a flower?
questions.explanation: Categoric data is descriptive - it is a label or category (like colours, plant species, or names). It is not a number.
questions.hint: Can you count the colours on a scale, or are they just different types/categories?

questions.q: Which of the following is a property of sound waves?
options: They can travel through a vacuum
They travel faster in air than in water
They do not require a medium to travel
questions.explanation: Sound waves are longitudinal waves where particles vibrate parallel to the direction of energy flow. They cannot travel through a vacuum and travel faster in denser media like water.
questions.hint: Think about whether sound needs something to travel through.

questions.q: Which statement best explains why specialised cells have many mitochondria?
options: They need more mitochondria to store food
They perform functions that require large amounts of energy
Mitochondria protect the cell from damage
Multiple mitochondria help cells divide faster
questions.explanation: Cells with high energy demands, like muscle cells and nerve cells, contain many mitochondria to produce sufficient ATP through aerobic respiration.
questions.hint: Think about which body cells work the hardest and need the most energy.

questions.q: Liquid → gas is called…
questions.explanation: Liquid to gas is evaporating, like water turning into steam when heated.
questions.hint: What happens to puddles on a sunny day?

questions.q: A block of ice is left in a warm room. Solid water particles vibrate more vigorously. What does this lead to?
options: The ice melts because forces between particles are overcome
The ice sublimates directly to gas
The particles move apart but remain solid
questions.explanation: As particles vibrate more due to heating, they eventually vibrate fast enough to overcome the forces holding them in fixed positions, causing melting.
questions.hint: What happens to forces between particles when they vibrate faster?

questions.q: When a gas is compressed into a smaller volume at constant temperature, what happens to the pressure?
options: Pressure decreases because particles have less space
Pressure increases because particles collide with walls more frequently
Pressure stays the same because temperature hasn't changed
questions.explanation: Compressing a gas reduces the volume, so particles hit the walls in a smaller space more frequently, increasing pressure even though temperature and particle speed remain constant.
questions.hint: How does reducing space affect how often particles hit the walls?

questions.q: In the periodic table, why do elements in Group 1 (alkali metals) have similar chemical properties?
options: They have the same number of neutrons
They have the same number of electrons in their outermost shell
They have the same atomic mass
They all form the same compounds
questions.explanation: Elements in the same group have the same number of electrons in their outermost shell, which determines their chemical behavior and reactivity patterns.
questions.hint: What feature of an atom determines how it reacts with other elements?

questions.q: In a stable ecosystem, what does the cycling of nutrients between biotic and abiotic components primarily depend on?
options: Decomposers breaking down dead material and returning elements to the soil
questions.explanation: Decomposers are essential for nutrient cycling because they break down dead organisms and waste, returning nutrients (like nitrogen and carbon) to the soil and atmosphere so producers can use them again.
questions.hint: What organism breaks down dead leaves and returns nutrients to soil?

questions.q: What is the chemical symbol for gold?
questions.explanation: Gold's symbol is Au, which comes from its Latin name 'aurum'.
questions.hint: The symbol comes from an old language name for gold.

questions.q: Two identical bulbs are connected in series to a battery. If one bulb fails and breaks the circuit, what happens to the other bulb?
questions.explanation: In a series circuit, if one component breaks, the circuit is broken and current cannot flow anywhere. Both bulbs go off.
questions.hint: In series, there is only one path for current to follow.

questions.q: What does a voltmeter measure?
questions.explanation: A voltmeter measures the potential difference (voltage) across a component in volts.
questions.hint: Volt — it's in the name!

questions.q: Cells become specialised to perform specific functions. What is this process called?
questions.explanation: Differentiation is when a cell changes to become specialised for a particular job (e.g., nerve cells, muscle cells).
questions.hint: It's when cells change to fit different jobs.

questions.q: A sample of pure copper has a mass of 64 g. How many moles of copper are present? (Relative atomic mass of Cu = 64)
questions.explanation: Number of moles = mass ÷ relative atomic mass = 64 ÷ 64 = 1 mole
questions.hint: Use the formula: moles = mass ÷ Ar

questions.q: A kettle has a power rating of 2000 W. Which statement about energy transfer is correct?
options: It transfers 2000 W of heat energy
It stores 2000 J in the water
questions.explanation: Power is measured in watts (W), where 1 W = 1 J/s. So 2000 W means 2000 joules of energy transferred every second.
questions.hint: Power = energy ÷ time. What is power measured in joules per?

questions.q: What is the chemical formula for a molecule containing 2 nitrogen atoms and 5 oxygen atoms?
questions.explanation: The subscript numbers show how many atoms of each element are present. 2 nitrogen atoms and 5 oxygen atoms is written as N₂O₅.
questions.hint: The number after each element symbol shows how many atoms of that element are in the molecule.

questions.q: An oscilloscope display shows two sound waves side by side. Wave X has waves that are closer together than Wave Y. What can you conclude?
options: Wave X is louder than Wave Y
Wave X has a shorter wavelength than Wave Y
Wave X travels faster than Wave Y
Wave Y has a higher frequency than Wave X
questions.explanation: Waves that are closer together have a shorter wavelength. Wavelength depends on how the waves are spaced, not on loudness, speed (in the same medium), or frequency directly.
questions.hint: Waves closer together means the distance between peaks is smaller—that's wavelength.

questions.q: A power station generates 1,000,000 joules of energy. If it took 1 minute to generate this energy, what was the power output?
questions.explanation: Power = Energy ÷ Time = 1,000,000 J ÷ 60 s = 16,667 W (approximately).
questions.hint: Power is measured in watts. 1 watt = 1 joule per second.

questions.q: In plant cells, the vacuole is mainly used for:
options: Storing water and maintaining cell shape
questions.explanation: The large central vacuole in plant cells stores water, nutrients, and helps the plant cell maintain its rigid shape and firmness.
questions.hint: It keeps the plant cell firm and stores cell sap.

questions.q: Which process produces variation in offspring?
questions.explanation: Sexual reproduction produces variation because genetic material from two different parents combines. Asexual reproduction produces identical offspring.
questions.hint: It involves two parents contributing genes.

questions.q: What does it mean to replicate an experiment?
options: To do the experiment multiple times
questions.explanation: Replication means repeating an experiment several times to check that your results are reliable and not due to chance.
questions.hint: Think about doing the same thing over and over again.

questions.q: Which component stores and releases electrical energy?
questions.explanation: A battery stores chemical energy and converts it into electrical energy to power a circuit.
questions.hint: Look for the portable source of power.

questions.q: A student measures the time it takes for sugar to dissolve in water at different temperatures. What is the independent variable?
options: The time taken for sugar to dissolve
questions.explanation: The independent variable is what you deliberately change. The student is changing the temperature to see how it affects dissolving time.
questions.hint: Ask yourself: 'What am I deliberately changing?' That is your independent variable.

questions.q: Two species of bird live in the same woodland and eat the same insects. Over time, one species begins to hunt only at night while the other hunts during the day. This is an example of:
questions.explanation: The two species are reducing competition by occupying different niches (time periods). Each species now feeds in a different way, allowing both to survive.
questions.hint: What has changed to reduce competition between them?

questions.q: Why do cells that line the small intestine have many finger-like projections called villi?
options: To increase surface area for absorption of nutrients
To help the intestine move food along
questions.explanation: The villi greatly increase the surface area of the epithelial tissue, allowing more efficient absorption of digested nutrients.
questions.hint: What advantage does a larger surface area give for absorption?

questions.q: What is the SI unit for measuring frequency?
questions.explanation: Frequency is measured in Hertz (Hz). It represents the number of waves passing a point per second.
questions.hint: Frequency tells us how many complete waves occur in one second.

questions.q: A drum is struck and produces a sound. Which of the following describes the motion of the drum head?
options: It moves in a straight line away from you
questions.explanation: When struck, the drum head oscillates (vibrates) up and down repeatedly, creating compressions and rarefactions in the air that form sound waves.
questions.hint: Think about what happens to the surface of a drum when you hit it.

questions.q: In which state of matter are the particles closest together?
options: All states have particles equally spaced
questions.explanation: Solid particles are closest together because they are tightly packed in fixed positions.
questions.hint: Which state takes up the least amount of space?

questions.q: A substance dissolves completely in water at room temperature. After cooling the solution in a freezer, crystals form at the bottom. What is this process called?
questions.explanation: Crystallisation is when a dissolved solid forms solid crystals as the solution cools or evaporates.
questions.hint: What happens when a solution becomes too concentrated for the dissolved solid to stay dissolved?

questions.q: In a food chain: grass → rabbit → fox → wolf, which organism has the least available energy?
questions.explanation: The wolf is at the highest trophic level. Energy decreases by approximately 90% at each step up the food chain, so top predators have the least energy available.
questions.hint: Think about which organism is furthest from the original energy source (the Sun).
